steps.title: Fastest path: graph or solve the equation
steps.content:
Type the equation into Desmos as written:
$$f \left(x\right)= \left(x-10\right) \left(x+13\right)$$Graph it and use the visible intercept, root, or vertex the question asks for. The requested value is $-\frac{3}{2}$.

steps.title: Find the vertex x-coordinate
steps.content:
A parabola opening upward reaches its minimum at the vertex, which lies midway between the roots:
$$x = \frac{10 + (-13)}{2} = \frac{-3}{2} = -1.5$$
